label: Punk Hazard fire-and-ice geographic system

atlas_anchor.warning: The atlas coordinate is deterministic placement, not canon longitude or a source for local bearings.

verified_relationships: The Burning Lands and Ice Lands are opposing halves separated by a central seawater crater lake.
The Burning Lands are southwest and the Ice Lands northeast; this compass orientation is chapter-safe from chapter 659.
The central lake connects to the sea and contains strong wind and currents caused by the temperature-pressure difference.
The cold side contains a river leading to a captured-ships harbor; icebergs obstruct the route.
The Third Research Institute is built into the base of a cold-side mountain with a natural harbor in front.
The ruined First and Second Research Institutes lie beyond the mountain behind the surviving third institute.
interpolated_relationships: Coastline outline, crater diameter, river curvature, harbor dimensions, mountain spacing, and exact laboratory footprint are artistic interpolation.
The local southwest/northeast axis is encoded without implying a canon globe bearing.
The volcano, melted street grid, ice mountain count, tanks, pipes, and iceberg placements are representative LOD1 geography.
topology.historical_state_policy: The hot/cold geography is static from its chapter reveal. The Akainu-Aokiji duel is referenced history disclosed in chapter 658, never an actor simulation or geography transformation. A chapter-658-only environmental-wave overlay may illustrate the explanation when explicitly labeled referenced_historical_reconstruction.

states.historical_reference: The prior ten-day duel explains the permanent split; no duel actors or transformation animation render.

states.state_delta: Southwest hot / northeast cold orientation becomes explicit.
The lake is identified as the duel epicenter later filled with seawater; geometry does not transform.

ambient_clip.name: punk_hazard_environment_cycle
ambient_clip.meaning: Neutral atmosphere cycle only: boundary rotation, steam breathing, lava-glow pulse, and ice-wind drift. It does not reenact the historical duel or move geography.

historical_memory_clip.name: punk_hazard_duel_memory_fx
historical_memory_clip.meaning: Abstract magma and ice environmental waves pass over the already-final island. There are no characters, no exact choreography claim, and no pre-duel geography state.

chapter_logic.default_rule: Render only components whose reveal_chapter is less than or equal to the reader chapter; before chapter 655 render no Punk Hazard model.
chapter_logic.unknown_gate_rule: Do not invent interior room visibility, exact bearings, or historical duel choreography.

visual_program.style: stylized LOD1 world theatre with readable fire/ice silhouette and original procedural materials

runtime_budget.texture_policy: procedural materials only; no external textures

coordinate_policy.warning: Never infer local geography from the atlas point or project the local SW/NE axis as a global bearing.

integration_contract.node_gate_policy: Read component_id, reveal_chapter, gate_confidence, and default_hidden from glTF extras.
integration_contract.ambient_policy: Sample punk_hazard_environment_cycle only while the model is visible and chapter >= 657; pause for reduced motion or when offscreen. Play punk_hazard_duel_memory_fx once only on chapter-658 entry and label it referenced_historical_reconstruction.

unresolved: Exact coastline and central-crater geometry are not available as canon measurements.
Exact river, harbor, laboratory, and destroyed-institute bearings are not established.

The specific chapter where gale-force boundary wind is first verbally explained needs panel-level verification; the neutral ambient visualization is gated to the visible two-sided discovery at chapter 657.
Interior Third Research Institute room topology is deliberately outside this geographic asset.
Historical duel choreography and character staging are deliberately excluded; the optional memory FX is abstract environmental reconstruction only.
